scope: generation job API, inline deterministic adapter, worker startup, K8s async smoke, worker outage/restore, and explicit async policy evidence

cases.evidence: 5 inline jobs returned succeeded status and redacted summaries
accepted inline job moved to failed with response_summary null and user-facing error_detail
queue failure returned HTTP 503 and marked the created job failed
two GET calls for the same job status returned identical payloads
worker waits for Redis readiness before constructing the RQ Worker
kind async overlay smoke passed Redis/RQ worker plus Postgres history
kind failure-injection smoke scaled deploy/worker to 0, observed the same job remain queued 3 times, restored worker to 1 replica, and observed final succeeded
POST /generation-jobs rejects reference_image_b64 until object storage and retention policy are selected
GET /generation-jobs/policy reports cancel unsupported, automatic_retries 0, worker_job_timeout_seconds null, and no dead-letter queue
POST /generation-jobs/{job_id}/cancel returns HTTP 501 with a bounded Korean detail
automatic retries, worker job timeout, and dead-letter queue are not claimed until a storage/retention policy is selected
RQ backend may run multiple workers, but exactly-once processing and worker affinity are not claimed
